title: Onboarding database service managing FTS5 index and progress tracking
purpose: Provides database operations for onboarding functionality including full-text search over topic content and user progress tracking with notes and team data.
module_context: Exports types for search results, users, progress entries, notes, and team members. Defines schema for progress database with tables for users, progress, notes, and path progress.

steps.content: Ensure the onboarding directory exists for storing database files.
Check if the pseudo database file exists before proceeding.
Determine if the index database needs to be rebuilt based on modification times.
IF rebuilding is needed, close any existing index database connection.
Open or reopen the index database file.
IF rebuilding is needed, drop the existing FTS5 table.
IF rebuilding is needed, create a new FTS5 table with file path, method name, and content fields.
IF rebuilding is needed, iterate through all files in the pseudo database.
IF rebuilding is needed, for each file, extract method steps and insert them into the FTS5 table.
Ensure the FTS5 index is up-to-date before performing search.
IF the index database is not available, return an empty result set.
Construct a SQL query to search the FTS5 table with the provided query.
Escape special FTS5 syntax in the query by wrapping it in double quotes.
IF a scope is provided, add file path filtering to the query.
Execute the search query and retrieve matching rows.
Map the query results to the expected search result format.
IF the progress database is already initialized, return it immediately.
Ensure the onboarding directory exists for storing database files.
Open or create the progress database file.
Execute the progress database schema to create necessary tables.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Insert a new user record into the users table with the provided name.
Return the created user with ID, name, and creation timestamp.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Query all users from the database ordered by name.
Map the database rows to the expected user format.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Query a user by ID from the database.
IF no user is found, return null.
ELSE map the database row to the expected user format.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Insert or replace a progress record for the user and file path with the specified status.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Delete the progress record for the specified user and file path.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Query all progress records for the specified user.
Map the database rows to the expected progress entry format.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Query all notes for the specified user and file path.
Map the database rows to the expected note format.

methods.name: addNote
methods.normalized_params: (userId: number, filePath: string, content: string): Note

steps.content: Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Insert a new note record for the user and file path with the provided content.
Return the created note with all its fields.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Update the content and updated timestamp of the note with the specified ID.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Delete the note with the specified ID from the database.
Ensure the progress database is initialized.
Query all users and their progress statistics to build team member data.
For each user, fetch their explored file paths.
Map the combined data to the expected team member format.
Attempt to get the pseudo database instance for the project.
IF the pseudo database is accessible, check if it contains any files.
IF the pseudo database is not accessible or has no files, return false.
ELSE return true indicating the pseudo database is ready.
IF an index database connection exists, close it and set the reference to null.
IF a progress database connection exists, close it and set the reference to null.
